enforced.mr_name: SAM RNEXT equals alias roundtrip invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the '=' alias is semantically equivalent to the explicit RNAME string per SAMv1 §1.4.7

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: error-consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on the malformed record; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.oracle: error-consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when FLAG 0x4 is set on a record with RNAME != '*' and POS > 0; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.oracle: error-consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when paired-end reads have same-signed TLEN values; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P clears mate-context bits (0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) when FLAG bit 0x1 is unset. SAMv1 §1.4 p47: these bits are only meaningful when 0x1 is set.

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

enforced.mr_name: SEQ case normalization preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — SEQ letter case is semantically irrelevant per SAM spec, so length(), aligned(), and reverse_complement() must return identical values after uppercasing SEQ.

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR. An `=' denotes the base is identical to the reference base. No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias canonicalization preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — RNEXT '=' is a spec-authorized synonym for RNAME, so target(), aligned(), and length() must return identical values after toggling between explicit and alias forms.

evidence.quote: RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. ... This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — shuffling optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields (columns 12+) does not affect the 11 mandatory fields or the parsed record's core alignment properties.

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

enforced.mr_name: Header @CO comment shuffling preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — @CO comment lines have no ordering semantics per SAM spec, so shuffling them must not affect parsed alignment record query results.

evidence.quote: @CO & One-line text comment. Unordered multiple @CO lines are allowed.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR split-or-merge preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves total query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths, so alignment properties are invariant.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header @HD subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled-subtag SAM produces identical data structures; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header @SQ record subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled-subtag SAM produces identical reference sequence dictionaries; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each @SQ line.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header @RG record subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled-subtag SAM produces identical read-group dictionaries; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each @RG line.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header @PG record subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled-subtag SAM produces identical program record dictionaries; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each @PG line.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM alignment optional tag field order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the permuted-tag-order SAM produces identical alignment records; the canonical normalizer sorts optional tags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header @CO comment line order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled-@CO SAM produces identical data; @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics, and the canonical normalizer sorts them deterministically.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that @CO lines have no ordering semantics, but @CO lines are excluded from the TAG:VALUE format and are free-text comments (regex /^@CO\t.*/), implying no ordering constraint.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header @SQ record order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the reordered-header SAM produces identical reference sequence dictionaries; the canonical normalizer sorts @SQ lines by SN tag so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that @SQ lines can appear in any order relative to each other, but the @HD line must be first. The canonical normalizer sorts @SQ lines by SN, implying order is not semantically significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag permutation invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after canonical normalization (sorted subtags within each header line)

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CIGAR split-or-merge invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — splitting 10M into 4M6M or merging 4M6M into 10M preserves total query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (advisory) not 'must', and the recommended practice discourages adjacent identical ops but does not forbid them; splitting/merging preserves the total consumed lengths

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: SEQ field letter case is semantically irrelevant per spec. Parsing uppercase and lowercase SEQ must yield identical sequence content. The canonical normalizer should produce the same output for both representations.

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' which means parsers should accept both cases, but some may not preserve case through round-trips.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR hard/soft clipping equivalence normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: Hard-clipping (H) and soft-clipping (S) at the ends of a CIGAR string represent different conventions for encoding clipped bases. Converting H->S (padding dummy bases in SEQ/QUAL) or S->H (trimming those bases) preserves the core alignment. The parsed alignment's mapped reference coordinates and the aligned portion of the query must remain identical.

evidence.quote: H can only be present as the first and/or last operation. S may only have H operations between them and the ends of the CIGAR string.

ambiguity_flags: H->S conversion requires adding dummy bases to SEQ/QUAL which changes the stored sequence; S->H conversion discards bases. Different tools may handle this differently.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: Zero-length CIGAR operations contribute nothing to the alignment and should be removed. After removal and merging of newly-adjacent same-type ops, the parsed alignment must represent the same alignment with identical query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops; the recommendation only says adjacent ops should be different. Some parsers may reject zero-length ops while others silently accept them.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag illegal type character rejection
quarantine.oracle: error-consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when an optional tag's type character is not one of A, i, f, Z, H, B; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The spec p27 chunk discusses adding new tags but does not explicitly enumerate the allowed type characters AifZHB in this chunk; the SAMtags §2.1 table (not retrieved) defines the regex [AifZHB] for the type field. The rule is well-established in practice but the retrieved chunk is advisory-level evidence.

quarantine.mr_name: POS+LN coordinate shift invariance with boundary check
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing @SQ LN by the same shift, the parsed alignment coordinates (POS, CIGAR reference consumption) remain consistent: POS + sum(M|=|X|D|N lengths) <= LN for every mapped record, and the relative ordering of records by coordinate is preserved. The shift is a uniform translation of the coordinate system that does not change the biological interpretation.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: LN* & Reference sequence length. Range: $[1,\,2^31-1]$

ambiguity_flags: The spec says POS 'should be between 1 and the @SQ header's LN value' (ADVISORY, not mandatory). The pos_shift_with_sq_ln_bound_check transform enforces the stronger invariant that POS+ref_consume <= LN, which is the Picard validation rule CIGAR_MAPS_OFF_REFERENCE.

quarantine.mr_name: BAM round-trip coordinate preservation
quarantine.oracle: After SAM→BAM→SAM round-trip, every alignment record's POS field (1-based coordinate) is unchanged from the original. The BAM binary format uses 0-based coordinates internally (per SAM spec §Terminologies and Concepts), so the round-trip exercises the 1-based↔0-based conversion. Any off-by-one error in the conversion would manifest as a ±1 shift in POS after the round-trip.

evidence.quote: 0-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is zero. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a half-closed-half-open interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [2,7). The BAM, BCFv2, BED, and PSL formats are using the 0-based coordinate system.

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines BAM as 0-based but does not mandate that SAM↔BAM conversion must preserve POS exactly; however, this is the universal expectation of all implementations.

quarantine.mr_name: CRAM round-trip coordinate preservation
quarantine.oracle: After SAM→CRAM→SAM round-trip, every alignment record's POS field (1-based coordinate) is unchanged from the original. CRAM uses a different coordinate encoding scheme (delta encoding, reference-based compression), so the round-trip exercises coordinate reconstruction from the CRAM codec.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may canonicalize CIGAR (e.g., M↔=/X conversion) which could affect the CIGAR field but not POS. The oracle should use the canonical normalizer's cram_safe mode to account for this.

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record coordinate normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: For records with FLAG bit 0x4 (unmapped) set, normalizing MAPQ to 255 does not change the coordinate semantics: POS and RNAME remain as originally specified. Per SAM spec, unmapped reads with RNAME='*' and POS=0 have no coordinate, while 'placed' unmapped reads (RNAME set, POS set) retain their placement coordinates. The coordinate indexing of the file is unaffected.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says unmapped reads 'should' have RNAME='*' and POS=0 (ADVISORY), but also allows 'placed' unmapped reads where RNAME and POS are filled in (see Indexing BAM §p264).

quarantine.mr_name: HD VN minor version bump round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P canonicalizes the VN field. Toggling between SAM 1.5 and 1.6 should not change parsed content since 1.6 only added clarifications.

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — SAM Version History] Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

ambiguity_flags: Some SUTs may switch behavior based on VN; the oracle must account for this

quarantine.mr_name: Header @HD subtag shuffling preserves query method results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — @HD TAG:VALUE pair order is unconstrained by the SAM spec, so shuffling them must not affect parsed alignment record query results.

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that @HD subtag order is unconstrained, but the table format lists tags without ordering semantics, and the canonical normalizer sorts them.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal preserves query method results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — removing zero-length CIGAR ops and merging adjacent identical ops preserves the total query-consume and reference-consume lengths, so alignment properties remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.6 doesn't forbid 0-length ops but the Recommended Practice says adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
